steps.title: Compare spread around the mean
steps.content:
Standard deviation measures how clustered the values are around the mean. The list with values most tightly packed around its center has the smallest SD.

A: 32, 33, 35, 37, 38 — spread $\pm 3$.
B: 33, 33, 35, 37, 37 — spread $\pm 2$.
C: 33, 34, 35, 36, 37 — spread $\pm 2$ (every step of 1).
D: 34, 35, 35, 35, 36 — spread $\pm 1$, with three values at the mean.

steps.title: Pick the tightest set
steps.content:
Set D has range only 2 and three of the five values exactly equal to the mean (35), giving the smallest deviations. Its standard deviation is clearly less than the others.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
